edible_parts: Nuts (only after thorough leaching); toxic raw.
other_uses: Spreading California tree with fragrant white flower spikes; drops early in summer drought.
prep: The large nuts were a staple only after slicing, boiling, and long leaching to remove toxic saponins/glycosides; otherwise all parts are poisonous. The crushed raw nuts were also used to stun fish.
risks: Toxic raw (saponins/aesculin); edible only after extensive leaching.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
